tspan.tspan: Central paths come from the study of linear optimization. These problems
consist of minimizing or maximizing a linear objective function with a finite
number of linear constraints. A general math mathmatical program is given
min{f(x): x\in X} or max{f(x): x\in X}
Of particular instrest are program that are:
- bounded - there exists a glb(f(X)) if minimizing or a lub(f(X)) if maximizing
- well-posed - if there is some x*, called the objective value, in X so that f(x*)
We call our primary mathmatical program the primal program or primal.
x: Ax + s = b, x\ge 0, s\ge 0}.
For each program there is an assoiated one call the dual program or dual,
These problems are linked together through weak duality which states that
the objective value of (P) must be less than or equal to that of (D). This
bounds the biggest possible value of the maximizer below the least
